title: Robust Crispy Peruvian Kippers Picarones
description: A crispy and savory Peruvian dish featuring kippers, perfect for a brunch mood, with a bitter and satisfying flavor, this satvik-friendly recipe is a must try. The picarones are a traditional Peruvian donut made from sweet potato and anise, but in this recipe, we will use a variation with kippers.
instructions: Preheat the oil in a deep frying pan to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
Mix the mashed sweet potatoes, anise seeds, flour, baking powder, and salt in a large bowl.
Add the kippers to the bowl and mix well.
Gradually add the water to the bowl and mix until a thick batter forms.
Using a spoon, drop small balls of the batter into the hot oil.
Fry the picarones until they are golden brown and crispy.
Remove the picarones from the oil and place them on a paper towel to drain excess oil.
Serve the picarones hot and enjoy.
